question:
Will Crewe Alexandra FC vs. Doncaster Rovers FC end in a draw?

Description: In the upcoming game, scheduled for November 1, 2025
If the game ends in a draw, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve to "Yes".

Resolution source: https://www.thefa.com/competitions/thefacup

evidences.content: Game Board may, in exceptional circumstances, approve the playing of drawn, postponed or abandoned matches on the following Saturday. in the Third, Fourth, Fifth and Sixth Rounds Proper on or before the following Saturday. (ii) When a replayed match is drawn after 90 minutes, an extra 30 minutes shall be played, and 15 minutes shall be played in each half. The Referee shall allow for time wasted or lost through accident or other cause. evidences.content: Team shall be permitted to compete for another Team in the current playing season. (f) Kick off times shall be between 10.00am and 2.00pm unless mutually agreed otherwise. (g) A Team may at its discretion and in accordance with the Laws of the Game use up to five substitute players who may be selected from up to five nominated to the Referee prior to the commencement of the tie. A player who has been substituted then becomes a substitute and may replace another player at any time subject to the substitution being carried out in accordance with Law 3 of the Laws of Association Football. (h) Ties which are postponed shall be [...] (f) Where play is not possible owing to causes beyond the control of either Team, the Match Officials shall be entitled to their travelling expenses. evidences.title: Josh March: Crewe striker out with hamstring injury - BBC Sport
evidences.content: "We have to control our emotions - how high they must have felt on Monday [after beating Harrogate] how well they played, and then to this, it's about us controlling those emotions," Bell added. "We need to analyse the reasons why we didn't get the performance we didn't get. "I keep going on about consistency and we need to get better. "If we can consistently churn out performances, results will follow." The Alex are back in action on Tuesday in their latest Vertu Trophy group game against Liverpool's under-21 side. Bell said he tie will give him the chance to make several changes and will be "an opportunity to get minutes into people." Crewe beat Chesterfield 7-1 in their opening match in the competition. ## Related topics ## Top stories [...] Bell did not know the severity of March's injury but was concerned he had done some damage to his hamstring. "It looks that way. You can see by the way he pulled up," he said. "He's been brilliant since he's been here, hopefully it's not that bad but, if it is, it gives an opportunity to one of the others." Back-to-back wins 'mean nothing' - Crewe boss Bell Crewe comeback great for confidence - Sanders Young players came 'out of the shado...
